name: SaltStack REST API (salt-api)
what_it_does: Event-driven infrastructure automation and configuration management with REST API (salt-api) for remotely executing commands, applying states, and managing configuration across hundreds to thousands of systems.
use_cases: Executing remote commands across multiple servers simultaneously from automation agents
Applying configuration states (packages, files, services) to maintain desired state
Querying minion (server) status, grains (system facts), and pillar data
Orchestrating multi-step infrastructure workflows via Salt orchestration runner
Triggering configuration rollbacks when deployment anomalies are detected
not_for: Organizations without Linux/Unix server infrastructure managed at scale
Simple single-server management where SSH suffices
best_when: An agent manages hundreds of bare-metal or VM servers requiring deterministic configuration management with real-time execution capability.
avoid_when: You're managing containerized workloads on Kubernetes or have fewer than 20 servers to manage.

auth.notes: eauth (external auth) system supports multiple backends: PAM, LDAP, file-based. Login returns token used for subsequent API calls. Tokens expire after configurable time.

pricing.paid_tiers: VMware Tanzu Saltstack (Aria Automation Config): enterprise support from $25/node/year
pricing.notes: Core SaltStack is Apache 2.0 open-source. VMware acquired SaltStack in 2020 and offers enterprise support. Open-source version is fully functional without commercial support.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted; limited by master server capacity and minion count

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation is comprehensive for core operations. Async job system requires polling for results. Error messages from remote minions can be verbose Python stack traces.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Salt state application is inherently idempotent — applying the same state twice produces the same result. Commands are not idempotent but states are.
known_agent_gotchas: All operations are async — job must be submitted and polled for completion with jid
Minion connectivity must be verified before commands — offline minions silently receive no commands
eauth login token must be renewed before expiry — agents need token refresh logic
Large minion target expressions can timeout if master is overwhelmed — use targeting filters

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS configurable (not default in all versions). eauth supports LDAP/PAM integration. AES encryption for Salt master-minion communication. Pillar system for secrets management. Peer configuration controls inter-minion permissions.
